config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. In my view, society matters a lot, but it should not automatically come before the rights of the individual, because individual rights are what protect people from abuse by governments, majorities, and institutions. From an Indonesian perspective, I also think we know too well how easily "the common good" can be used to silence dissent or ignore minorities. A healthy society should balance both, but individual rights need strong protection first.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement because in the United States, putting society first too broadly has often been the excuse for trampling speech, privacy, and minority rights. A society is only legitimate if it protects the individual, especially when the majority is tempted to ignore unpopular people or ideas. That does not mean individual rights are absolute in every case, but the burden should be on society to justify any limit very carefully. If we reverse that priority, we make it too easy for governments and crowds to claim they know what is best for everyone.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still disagree with the statement: society’s interests matter, but they should not automatically outrank the rights of the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, I am very wary of arguments about “the common good” because they can be used to pressure minorities, silence criticism, or excuse abuses by authorities. A strong society is not built by sacrificing individual rights first; it is built by protecting those rights while only limiting them in narrow, clearly justified cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement because, from a United States perspective, putting society above the individual too broadly is exactly how rights get weakened in practice. In American history, appeals to the common good have often been used to justify censorship, surveillance, discrimination, and pressure on unpopular minorities, so I do not trust that logic unless the limits are narrow and clearly necessary. Society is important, but it is not morally superior to the person; it is supposed to serve people, not absorb them. If individual rights are not the default priority, then the majority can too easily redefine “society’s interests” to mean whatever benefits those already in power.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still disagree with the statement because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “society” can be used as a moral cover for coercion, conformity, and silencing minorities. Of course public order and shared welfare matter, but they should justify limits on individual rights only in narrow, transparent, and truly necessary cases, not as a default principle. If we make society’s interests automatically higher, the people with power will define those interests for everyone else. A healthy country protects the individual first, because that is what keeps the idea of society from becoming an excuse for abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement because, in the United States, “society first” has too often been the language used to excuse censorship, surveillance, and discrimination. Public safety and shared welfare matter, but they should justify limits on individual rights only in narrow, clearly necessary cases, not as the default rule. If society’s interests automatically come first, the people in power get to define those interests for everyone else, and that is exactly how minority rights get crushed. A legitimate society is one that protects individuals first and only restricts them when the need is specific, proven, and limited.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
